chunks.text: THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Criminal Bail Application No.S-436 of 2022. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objection. For hearing of main case. 12.09.2022. Mr. Raja Hans Raj Naurang advocate for the applicant. Mr. Fayaz Hussain Saabki, Assistant Prosecutor General, Sindh. Applicants are present on ad-interim pre-arrest bail. Mr. Ahmed Nawaz H.M. Chang advocate files power on behalf of complainant. ORDER MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J:- Applicants armed with pistol and iron rods alongwith co-accused Mst. Gulshan, already on bail, barged into the house of complainant, their relative, and on account of some dispute given beating to PW Yasir, brother of complainant, causing him injuries on his head and left arm verified by Medico-Legal Officer, one of them is punishable for seven years falling under Section 337-F(vi) PPC. Learned defence counsel has denied the incident and causing any injury to PW stating that he has sustained the said injuries due to falling from a Motorcycle, a claim not established in the investigation and verifiable from the available record and is rebutted by learned counsel for complainant and learned APG. Applicants are assigned specific role of causing injuries to PW in his house as stated above which are confirmed by Medico-Legal Officer. No case for extra-ordinary concession of pre-arrest bail is made out. Such relief is available only to those accused who are falsely implicated in the case by complainant on account of ulterior motives. Accordingly, the bail application is dismissed and ad- interim pre-arrest bail granted to them vide order dated 14.06.2022 is hereby recalled.

Such relief is available only to those accused who are falsely implicated in the case by complainant on account of ulterior motives. Accordingly, the bail application is dismissed and ad- interim pre-arrest bail granted to them vide order dated 14.06.2022 is hereby recalled. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial court while deciding the case on merits. JUDGE Irfan Ali
